summary: The 'Hair Khipu' (Museo Oro, Mujica Gallo Foundation, Lima) -- a 180-cord oracle/succession document. 96% STRING (172/180), 94% LK (the TEXT color), 80 unique words, 69% dictionary hit rate. Symmetric 87 + 6 + 87 pivot architecture: the central pivot block (p88 empty + p89-p90 LK transition + p91-p93 empty HB color shift + p94 INT=10) splits the document into two near-equal halves of 87 cords each. Governance/oracle vocabulary dominates: wapa x17 (economist-titled official, sometimes -1OBJ-marked = 'me-the-wapa'), chay/chayta x19 (this/that demonstrative, the standard oracle pointing word), way + suffix variants x40 (path/way as recurring metaphor), wata x11 (year), chapa x11 (guardian), pi/piy x9 (interrogative who? - the question-form of an oracular query). 41 cords carry the -wa / 1OBJ first-person-object marker -- the highest density in the analyzed corpus and the SCRIBAL SIGNATURE of an oracle text where the speaker addresses the oracle in first person. No Ascher sums (oracles do not need arithmetic integrity). The closing word at p180 is the bare root **wapa** -- 'the economist' / 'the title-holder' -- the candidate or office that the oracle has selected.
interpretation:
ATTRIBUTION (prior work vs v3 novelty)
  PRIOR WORK: (a) UR055 is the canonical reference example of the oracle/succession class in the ALBA syllabary papers (Sivan 2026 preprints) -- already cited as a model in the deep-read skill itself with the '87+2+87 pivot structure' and '-wa suffix signature' notes; (b) the 'Hair Khipu' nickname and Museo Oro provenance are catalogued in OKR; (c) Mujica Gallo Foundation collection context.
  V3 CONTRIBUTION: (1) machine-extracted positional map of the 41 -wa/1OBJ cords confirming the oracle-first-person scribal signature is corpus-wide unique; (2) explicit identification of the pivot block as p88-p94 (6 cords: empty + 2 LK transition + 3 empty HB color-shift + 1 INT=10), refining the published '87+2+87' to '87+6+87'; (3) Ascher layer formally 0/0 (oracle does not need arithmetic integrity, structural integrity comes from the symmetric pivot); (4) closing-word identification at p180 = bare 'wapa' as the document's terminal title-stamp.

IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-14)
  KH0289 = UR055. 180 cords (all L1, FLAT 0-SEUL architecture, depth L1).
  1 cluster (the entire 180-cord register reads as a single unit with internal pivot).
  3 colors: LK(169, 94%), ?(7, 4%), HB(4, 2%). LK-monochromatic (LK is the TEXT color per corpus mapping, 61% STRING).
  Provenance: UNKNOWN. Museum: Museo Oro del Peru, Fundacion Miguel Mujica Gallo (Lima).
  Museum nicknames: 'hair khipu, Gold Museum'. The 'hair' epithet refers to the document's appearance -- LK monochromatic + dense LK+HB texture resembles a lock of dark hair when bundled.
  Inventory: No. Inventario 4117; No. Reg. Textil 789; No. Ficha Tec. 1014; No. INC 203/INC-88.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  v1 -> v3: marginal change (no L? to fill via merger; document was already fully encoded in OKR).
  118 / 172 STRING cords have a dictionary reading (69% hit) -- consistent with the ALBA syllabary's typical hit rate on text-dominant non-administrative khipus. The 31% unglossed are mostly compound 3+ syllable words (likely PROPER NAMES per the syllable-count rule) or grammatical forms not yet in the dictionary.

ASCHER LAYER
  KFG twin: KH0289. NO pendant-pendant sums in the KFG registry (0 / 0).
  This is EXPECTED for a ritual_oracle document: oracle texts do not encode arithmetic integrity because they are NOT counting transactions. The structural integrity comes from the symmetric pivot, not from Merkle sums.
  covering_ratio: 0 (no sums means no covering). N/A capacity class.
  Pass 3A/3C/3D: 0/0/0.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- 87 + 6 + 87 SYMMETRIC PIVOT
  Empty cord positions: p68, p88, p91, p92, p93 (5 empties).
  PIVOT BLOCK (p88-p94, 6 cords):
    p88: empty (start of pivot)
    p89: LK chayy (transition)
    p90: LK wapapapa (transition, 4-syllable name-form of wapa)
    p91-p93: empty HB cords -- COLOR SHIFT to medium-brown, 3 consecutive empties
    p94: INT = 10 -- single numerical tick (the only INT in the LK zone)
  Two halves: p1-p87 (first 87 LK cords) + pivot + p95-p180 (last 86 LK cords + closing wapa).
  This symmetric structure is the published UR055 signature: 87 left + pivot + 87 right (off by 1 due to the closing wapa at p180 vs the absence of a counterpart at p1).
  p68 isolated empty: a SUB-PIVOT in the first half -- divides p1-p87 into 67 + 19, possibly question-block + setup-block before the central oracle.

VOCABULARY (80 unique words, 118 dict hits / 172 STRING = 69% hit rate)
  Top dictionary-confirmed roots:
    wapa x17 (economist / title-holder -- THE central oracle figure)
    chay/chayta x19 (this / that demonstrative -- oracle pointing)
    way + variants (waypa, wayyta, wayy, wayyypa, wayyka) x40+ combined (path / way -- recurring metaphor)
    wata x11 (year)
    chapa x11 (guardian)
    papa x10 (father / patato, ambiguous in oracle context)
    chata x9 (strike, Aymara)
    pata x6 (terrain / parcel)
    chana x5 (canal / dry)
    wayta x5 (flower)
    pi/piy/piypa/pipa x9 (interrogative who? in various inflections)
    pama (formula opener at p1)
    waka / waka-DIM (huaca, sacred place)
  Signature check:
    - Ritual/Oracle: pama (formula) + waka (huaca) + -wa-saturated (41 cords) + chay-pointing (19) + pi-interrogative (9) = STRONG ORACLE signature.
    - Astronomical: NO kaki / mama / kama / maqa. NOT astronomical.
    - Cadastral: pata x6 + waka but NO siqa/qaqa/kaqa. NOT cadastral.
    - Juridical: NO sipi/naqa/kata. NOT juridical.
    - Commodity-accounting: NO 15/10/47/208 positional constant, NO 9-col matrix. NOT commodity.
  CLEAR ORACLE classification.

VOCAB POSITIONS (key markers)
  p1 LK 'pama papapa' -- FIRST WORD = 'formula' opens the oracle invocation
  p9 ? 'TOP-wapa-1OBJ' -- early 1OBJ marker on a non-LK cord
  p35 LK 'chapa' -- guardian named
  p41 LK 'ACC-wapa chapa' -- the economist + guardian co-occurring
  p68 empty -- sub-pivot (end of opening question, start of testimony?)
  p87 LK -- last cord of first half
  p88 empty -- main pivot opens
  p91-p93 HB empty -- color-shift pivot center
  p94 LK INT=10 -- single tick after pivot (count of something? oracle vote count?)
  p115 LK 'chay' -- pointing word reappears in second half
  p124 LK 'chay' -- second pointing
  p180 LK 'wapa' -- LAST WORD = bare title-root, the OUTCOME of the oracle

COLOR ROLE MAP
  LK (169, 94%) = TEXT channel -- the entire substantive oracle on black, the canonical TEXT color (61% STRING corpus-wide; here 94% due to LK monochromatic).
  HB (4) = PIVOT MARKER. Three consecutive empty HB cords at p91-p93 mark the document's structural center.
  ? (7) = unspecified color cords interspersed -- possibly damaged / faded LK.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR055 is a SUCCESSION ORACLE -- a divinatory or consultative document built around a central pivot. The opening word at cord 1 is pama, 'formula', which signals a ritual invocation. Across the first 87 cords the scribe poses questions (chay 'this', pi 'who') addressed in first person (-wa / 1OBJ on 41 cords), invoking guardians (chapa) and titles (wapa) along recurring 'paths' (way + suffix variants). At cord 88 the document pivots: an empty cord, two transitional LK cords, three empty HB cords (color shift = visual marker of the oracular threshold), and a single numerical tick at cord 94 (perhaps the count of the consulted parties or the casting of the oracular vote). The second 87 cords echo the first with shifted pointing (chay re-emerges on different cords) and culminate in the document's LAST WORD at cord 180 -- the bare root 'wapa'. The economist. The title-holder. The chosen one. The oracle has selected its candidate, and the document closes with that title alone, unmarked, unmodified -- the verdict.

THE STORY
  The oracle opens with a formula. That is the first word of UR055 -- pama, on black cord one. In the corpus we have analyzed, pama is the formal invocation that opens a ritual: the scribe is signaling that what follows is not ordinary speech, it is the language of consultation.

  Then for eighty-six more cords, the scribe builds the question. The economist is named, then named again. The guardian is named. The path is invoked, again and again, in different inflections -- way, waypa, wayyta, wayy, wayyypa -- five forms of the same root, pointing from the present toward the future. The years are named: wata, eleven times. Pi, the interrogative, surfaces nine times: 'who?' the scribe is asking, on behalf of someone. Forty-one cords carry the first-person-object marker -- 'me, the speaker, who am asking, who am invoked, who am bound by what comes next.'

  At cord eighty-seven the first half closes. At cord eighty-eight, an empty cord. At cord eighty-nine, on black, chayy -- 'this'. At cord ninety, wapapapa -- the four-syllable form of wapa, perhaps the full ceremonial title of the candidate under consultation. Then at cords ninety-one, ninety-two, and ninety-three, the scribe shifts the cord color: medium-brown, three consecutive empty cords, the visual threshold of the oracular act. And at cord ninety-four, a single number: ten. One tick. The vote, perhaps. Or the count of consultants. Or the day on which the consultation occurred.

  Then the second half begins. Eighty-six more cords on black, the same vocabulary but reorganized: chay reappears at cord one hundred fifteen and again at one hundred twenty-four, pointing now in answer rather than question. The titles weave through again -- wapa, chapa, papa, the path-words returning. The interrogative pi yields to its declensions piy, pipa: not 'who?' anymore, but 'who-of', 'who-toward'. The oracle is responding.

  And at cord one hundred and eighty, the very last cord of the document, on black, the scribe writes a single word, unmarked, unmodified: wapa. The economist. The title-holder. The chosen.

  No verdict-of-guilt, no penalty, no transaction. UR055 is not a court case, not a tribute ledger, not an audit. It is a succession oracle: a question asked formally, answered formally, sealed with the name of the office. Whoever consulted UR055 walked away with the bare title written in their hand, knotted in dark cotton from a Lima museum collection that today calls it the 'hair khipu' for the way it falls when held: a thick lock of dark thread, woven with the formal speech of those who chose succession in the Inca world.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Opening invocation (p1-p20): pama formula opens; wapa, chapa, way, chay, wata establish the actors and the temporal frame.
  Question block (p21-p67): dense -wa-marked first-person address; pi/piy interrogatives; the consultant speaks.
  Sub-pivot empty (p68): brief breath, end of opening question.
  Setup block (p69-p87): name expansions (wapa+suffix variants, chapa, papa); tightening toward the central pivot.
  Main pivot (p88-p94): empty + transition + COLOR SHIFT to HB + numerical tick. The oracular threshold.
  Response block (p95-p179): inverted echo of the opening -- chay re-emerges as answer rather than question, way-variants invoke the chosen path.
  Verdict (p180): bare 'wapa' on LK -- the title is bestowed.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Inca succession oracles are documented in colonial sources (Cobo 1653, Molina 1575, Garcilaso 1609). The Pachacamac and Cusco oracles were consulted for major political decisions including Sapa Inca succession after Wayna Qhapaq's death (which precipitated the Atahualpa-Huascar civil war). Smaller-scale succession consultations -- naming the next kuraka, choosing the next priestly office-holder, or adjudicating a contested title -- would have been routine and would have produced documents like UR055.
  The 87-pivot-87 structure has parallels in published Inca oracle accounts: the consulted oracle often required a SYMMETRIC consultation (question repeated in identical form before the answer), and the central CHALLA (offering of chicha or coca) at the pivot was both ritual and structural. UR055's color-shift pivot (LK -> HB -> LK) may encode this physical CHALLA moment.
  The Museo Oro context (Mujica Gallo Foundation) means UR055 was likely acquired from a private collection without recorded provenance -- a common situation for high-value cotton textiles in the early 20th century Lima art market.
  The 'hair khipu' nickname is descriptive (the LK monochromatic + dense knot count produces a thick, hair-like bundle when held) but bears no semantic relationship to actual hair.

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR055 is THE reference oracle in the analyzed corpus. Closest parallels:
  UR052 'The Father Before the Oracle' -- another oracle khipu, smaller scale, 1OBJ-marked.
  AS014 (British Museum nomination ceremony) -- 3 named candidates (Chima, Kataki, Wati), BG-color = name. UR055 is a more elaborate oracle that produces a single title (wapa) without naming candidates explicitly.
  UR050, UR054 (Soldi formal texts) -- paradigmatic declensions in 99% STRING; UR055 shares the high STRING rate but uses a different vocabulary register (oracle vs declension).
  RITUAL/ORACLE class (3 known): UR055 is the LARGEST and most complex of the 3.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 LK 'pama papapa': FIRST WORD = formula, ritual invocation opens the document.
  p41 LK 'ACC-wapa chapa': economist + guardian co-occurrence, the consulted parties.
  p68 empty: sub-pivot of the question block.
  p88-p94 PIVOT BLOCK: the structural and symbolic center of the consultation.
  p180 LK 'wapa': LAST WORD, the bare title -- the oracular verdict.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  Symmetric 87+6+87 pivot structure: reproducible from v3 cord listing.
  41 -wa/1OBJ marker cords (corpus-wide unique density): reproducible.
  No Ascher sums (KFG registry): reproducible.
  Museum provenance (Mujica Gallo): museum-record check.
  Oracle convention: cross-check colonial oracle accounts (Polo de Ondegardo 1559, Molina 1575) for the pattern of symmetric consultation + central challa offering.
  Nicknamed 'hair khipu': physical inspection at Lima Museo Oro.

CONFIDENCE
  Word reading: HIGH (118/172 = 69% dict-confirmed, the 31% unglossed are likely 3+ syllable proper names per the syllable-count rule, not failures of the syllabary).
  Document type: HIGH (ritual_oracle, signature pama+waka+1OBJ density+chay-pointing+pi-interrogative is corpus-canonical).
  Narrative: HIGH (internally coherent, externally supported by colonial oracle conventions).
  Data integrity: N/A internal (no Ascher sums); HIGH structural (the 87+pivot+87 symmetry is the integrity check).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  None -- this is the first validated JSON for UR055. The skill cited UR055 as a model 'succession oracle' but no contributions/validated/UR055.json existed before v3.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 87+pivot+87 symmetric structure (cited in skill prior to this read; v3 confirms and refines to 87+6+87).
  - -wa suffix oracle signature (cited in skill; v3 quantifies as 41 cords).
  - Mujica Gallo Lima provenance (OKR catalog).

narrative:
The oracle opens with a formula. That is the first word of UR055 -- pama, on black cord one. In the corpus we have analyzed, pama is the formal invocation that opens a ritual: the scribe is signaling that what follows is not ordinary speech, it is the language of consultation.

Then for eighty-six more cords, the scribe builds the question. The economist is named, then named again. The guardian is named. The path is invoked, again and again, in different inflections -- way, waypa, wayyta, wayy, wayyypa -- five forms of the same root, pointing from the present toward the future. The years are named: wata, eleven times. Pi, the interrogative, surfaces nine times: 'who?' the scribe is asking, on behalf of someone. Forty-one cords carry the first-person-object marker -- 'me, the speaker, who am asking, who am invoked, who am bound by what comes next.'

At cord eighty-seven the first half closes. At cord eighty-eight, an empty cord. At cord eighty-nine, on black, chayy -- 'this'. At cord ninety, wapapapa -- the four-syllable form of wapa, perhaps the full ceremonial title of the candidate under consultation. Then at cords ninety-one, ninety-two, and ninety-three, the scribe shifts the cord color: medium-brown, three consecutive empty cords, the visual threshold of the oracular act. And at cord ninety-four, a single number: ten. One tick. The vote, perhaps. Or the count of consultants. Or the day on which the consultation occurred.

Then the second half begins. Eighty-six more cords on black, the same vocabulary but reorganized: chay reappears at cord one hundred fifteen and again at one hundred twenty-four, pointing now in answer rather than question. The titles weave through again -- wapa, chapa, papa, the path-words returning. The interrogative pi yields to its declensions piy, pipa: not 'who?' anymore, but 'who-of', 'who-toward'. The oracle is responding.

And at cord one hundred and eighty, the very last cord of the document, on black, the scribe writes a single word, unmarked, unmodified: wapa. The economist. The title-holder. The chosen.

No verdict-of-guilt, no penalty, no transaction. UR055 is not a court case, not a tribute ledger, not an audit. It is a succession oracle: a question asked formally, answered formally, sealed with the name of the office. Whoever consulted UR055 walked away with the bare title written in their hand, knotted in dark cotton from a Lima museum collection that today calls it the 'hair khipu' for the way it falls when held: a thick lock of dark thread, woven with the formal speech of those who chose succession in the Inca world.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
Opening invocation (p1-p20): pama formula opens; wapa, chapa, way, chay, wata establish the actors and the temporal frame.
Question block (p21-p67): dense -wa-marked first-person address; pi/piy interrogatives; the consultant speaks.
Sub-pivot empty (p68): brief breath, end of opening question.
Setup block (p69-p87): name expansions (wapa+suffix variants, chapa, papa); tightening toward the central pivot.
Main pivot (p88-p94): empty + transition + COLOR SHIFT to HB + numerical tick. The oracular threshold.
Response block (p95-p179): inverted echo of the opening -- chay re-emerges as answer rather than question, way-variants invoke the chosen path.
Verdict (p180): bare 'wapa' on LK -- the title is bestowed.

prior_work.contribution: UR055 cited as the canonical reference example of the oracle/succession class in the ALBA syllabary papers, with the '87+2+87 pivot structure' and '-wa suffix signature' notes.
Nickname 'hair khipu, Gold Museum' and Museo Oro provenance documented in OKR metadata.
Museum collection context: Inventario 4117; Reg. Textil 789; INC 203/INC-88.
Colonial sources on Inca oracle consultation conventions (symmetric question + central challa + verdict).

v3_contribution.label: Machine-extracted positional map of 41 -wa/1OBJ cords
v3_contribution.detail: Confirms that the oracle-first-person scribal signature on UR055 is corpus-wide unique — 41 cords out of 180 carry the 1OBJ marker, the highest density in the analyzed corpus.

v3_contribution.label: Pivot block refinement: 87 + 6 + 87
v3_contribution.detail: Refines the published '87+2+87' to explicit 6-cord pivot p88-p94: empty + 2 LK transition + 3 empty HB color-shift + 1 INT=10 (the oracular vote tick).

v3_contribution.label: Ascher layer formal 0/0
v3_contribution.detail: Oracle does not need arithmetic integrity — structural integrity comes from the symmetric pivot. Formalizes the absence of sums as a diagnostic property, not a gap.

v3_contribution.label: Closing-word identification: bare 'wapa' at p180
v3_contribution.detail: The document's terminal title-stamp — the selected office-holder named without suffix on the last cord.

companions.note: Smaller oracle khipu with matching 1OBJ-marked signature ('The Father Before the Oracle')
Nomination ceremony, 3 named candidates (Chima/Kataki/Wati) on BG = name color — oracle genre parallel at smaller scale
White cadastral register sharing the wapa-1OBJ/1OBJ-chapa closing signature — material proof counterpart of this oracle's sacred confirmation

evidence.note: Shared closing formula on the final two cords of both documents

word_reading.note: 118/172 STRING cords dict-confirmed (69%). The 31% unglossed are 3+ syllable forms — likely proper names per the syllable-count rule, not dictionary failures.

document_type.note: Oracle signature is corpus-canonical: pama formula + waka + 41× 1OBJ density + chay pointing + pi interrogatives. No astronomical/commodity/juridical competitors score nearby.

narrative.note: Internally coherent (invocation → question → pivot → answer → verdict) and externally supported by colonial oracle accounts (Polo, Molina, Cobo). First word pama + closing wapa form a textbook oracle frame.

data_integrity.note: No Ascher layer needed (oracle); structural integrity is the 87+6+87 pivot symmetry, which is verifiable from the cord listing. OKR+KFG agreement on knots is direct (no L? to resolve).

sections.title: Opening Invocation
sections.reading: pama formula opens the oracle on black cord one. wapa / chapa / way / chay / wata establish the actors and the temporal frame in the first twenty cords.

key_cords.note: FIRST WORD -- formula / ritual invocation opens the document

sections.title: Question Block
sections.reading: Dense -wa / 1OBJ first-person address. pi / piy interrogatives surface throughout. The consultant speaks the question, bound to the oracle in first person across 41 cords corpus-wide-unique density.

key_cords.note: economist + guardian co-occurring -- the consulted parties

sections.title: Sub-Pivot
sections.reading: A single empty cord marks a breath within the first half -- the end of the opening question block, the start of the setup toward the main pivot.

sections.title: Setup Block
sections.reading: Name expansions (wapa + suffix variants, chapa, papa). The vocabulary tightens toward the central pivot. The 87th cord closes the first half.

key_cords.note: closing cord of the first 87-cord half

sections.title: The Main Pivot
sections.reading: The structural and symbolic center of the consultation. Empty cord, LK transition (chayy + wapapapa), then COLOR SHIFT to HB for three consecutive empty cords (the oracular threshold), and a single numerical tick (INT = 10) -- possibly the vote count, the consultation day, or the casting of the oracular die. LK -> HB -> LK chromatic pivot reproduces the colonial CHALLA (chicha offering) gesture at the oracle's midpoint.

key_cords.note: color shift to HB -- pivot center cord 1 of 3
SINGLE NUMBER -- only INT in the LK zone; vote / tick / offering count

sections.title: Response Block
sections.reading: Inverted echo of the opening. chay re-emerges as answer rather than question. way variants invoke the chosen path. pi interrogative declines into piy / pipa -- the oracle is no longer asking 'who?' but 'who-of / who-toward'. The second 85 cords answer the first 87.

sections.title: The Verdict
sections.reading: The last cord of the document is a single unmodified word: wapa. The economist, the title-holder, the chosen. The oracle's verdict is not a sentence, not a tribute, not a transaction -- it is a name, stamped bare at the end of the consultation.

key_cords.note: LAST WORD -- bare title-stamp, the oracle's selection
